name: Hibernation Bay C-7
description: A small chamber with four hibernation pods arranged in a square. Two are sealed and frosted over, their occupants dimly visible through the glass. One stands empty, its lid raised, interior dry. The fourth — yours — is open and still trailing cold vapor. Emergency strip lighting casts everything in pale amber. A status panel on the wall blinks. The air smells faintly of antiseptic.
secret: The empty pod still has power and could theoretically put someone back into hibernation if they climbed in and activated the sequence.

ai.prompt: This is the starting room. The goal is to introduce the player gently — a small, contained space that orients them before they step into the larger ship. Keep it simple: the pod they woke from, a couple of sealed pods with sleeping people (context, not interactive), and the status panel. Don't add too much here — the interesting stuff is out in the ship.

name: Corridor C
description: A broad corridor with gently curved walls of brushed metal. Colored guide strips run along the floor — green toward the bow, red toward the stern. Recessed lighting panels overhead flicker intermittently, some dead entirely. A faded mural on one wall depicts people planting trees under a stylized sun. Below it, someone has scratched smaller figures in a different style.
secret: The scratched figures below the mural are arranged in a specific pattern — they are actually a map of the deck drawn by a later generation of inhabitants.

ai.prompt: The first step into the wider ship. The player should start noticing that this place has layers — the mural and the scratched figures hint that different people lived here at different times. This corridor connects the safe starting area to the rest of the deck. Objects here should be transitional: signage, wall markings, small hints of the ship's history.

name: Junction C
description: Corridors branch in several directions from this hexagonal junction. A large directory console is mounted on the central pillar, its screen cracked but still displaying a partial deck map. The ceiling is higher here, and the air moves — somewhere a ventilation system still works. A bench built into the wall has been repurposed as a small shrine, covered in faded cloth and tiny carved figures.
secret: The shrine figures can be rearranged, and certain arrangements cause the directory console to display hidden deck sections that are otherwise marked RESTRICTED.

ai.prompt: This is the hub where the world opens up. The player should feel like there are many directions to go, each promising something different. The shrine is the first real cultural artifact — evidence that people here built meaning and ritual into their daily spaces. New content here should reinforce the sense of choice and branching possibility.

name: Medical Station C
description: A compact automated clinic adjacent to the hibernation bays. A diagnostic bed with a curved scanner arm sits in the center. Supply cabinets line one wall, most sealed with magnetic locks. A terminal on a swivel arm displays your vital signs — apparently it has been tracking you since you woke. The readout says SUBJECT: UNKNOWN. COGNITIVE BASELINE: PENDING.
secret: The cognitive assessment is real and functional — completing it might restore fragments of the player’s lost memory and identity.

ai.prompt: This room establishes the player's situation: they have no identity, no memory, and the ship's systems don't know who they are. It's functional and clinical but should hint that the medical technology has been maintained by automated systems rather than people. New objects here should be medical or diagnostic, and might reveal small fragments of information about the player or the ship.

name: Hydroponics Gallery
description: What was once an orderly growing facility has become a dense, humid tangle. Grow-lights blaze overhead on ancient timers, feeding plants that have long outgrown their troughs. Vines climb the walls and drape from irrigation pipes. The air is thick and green-smelling. Fruit hangs from branches that were never meant to exist on a spaceship. Somewhere water drips steadily into a pool.
secret: Some of the fruit is edible with surprising effects — mild euphoria, heightened senses, or vivid waking dreams. The dripping water has trace nutrients from centuries of botanical runoff.

ai.prompt: This is the first area that should really surprise the player — a vivid contrast to the clinical hibernation area. Life has been running wild here. The plants are the stars: strange, overgrown, bred into forms that feel almost alien. This is where the player first encounters the ship's creative divergence in a visceral way. New objects should be botanical, strange, or evidence of the communities who tended these gardens for centuries. Lean into the wonder and weirdness of what a thousand years of plant breeding produces.

name: Corridor C, Sternward
description: The corridor continues sternward, guide strips dimming with distance. Doors to either side are sealed and identical. The lighting gave up somewhere ahead.

name: Hibernation Bay C-8
description: Another bay, like yours, four pods in a square. All four are sealed and frosted, their status lights ticking green in unison. Nobody here is waking up today.
secret: The pods' occupants entered hibernation calmly and in order — the bay log, if accessed, shows a scheduled shutdown, not an emergency.

name: The Trough Rows
description: The orderly edge of the Botanical Quarter: grow-troughs in disciplined rows, each labeled with a brass cultivar tag. The labels are the only extravagant thing here. A tender robot works the rows on a fixed circuit, pruning with more ceremony than seems necessary.

name: The Deep Growth
description: The grow-lights failed here a long time ago, and the plants adapted by providing their own. Bioluminescent veins map the canopy in slow pulses. The path is a negotiation. Fruit glows at intervals overhead like a settlement seen from altitude, and something in the understory reorients its leaves toward you as you pass.
secret: The plants here are one organism wearing many shapes — the breeding program's final cultivar, still following its last instruction: diversify.

scenery.description: The light moves through the canopy in slow waves, always the same direction — bow to stern. If it's circulation, the heart is somewhere aft.
scenery.rejection: The canopy is well overhead, and the understory disapproves of climbing.

name: Commons Passage
description: A wide, dark passage. The guide strips here were painted over with welcome murals, and the murals have faded back to almost-strips. Dust softens every edge.

name: The Commons Hall
description: A hall built to hold a generation at once. Tiered seating descends toward a central floor of worn mosaic, and faction banners hang the height of three decks, colors gone gentle with age. Every table and bench is under a dust cloth, laid carefully, corners squared — somebody closed this room like a casket. The dark isn't total: one corner, far off, holds a steady warm light.
secret: The dust cloths were laid by the last person out, in a single night, alone. One cloth covers nothing at all — the table beneath was already gone, and the shape it preserves is a guess.

scenery.description: Seventeen banners, one per faction at the time of hanging. Several factions merged, two schismed, and one banner has been quietly turned to face the wall.
scenery.rejection: The banners hang three decks up, beyond reach and possibly beyond opinion.

scenery.description: The mosaic maps the ship's route, star by star, laid before launch. Foot traffic has worn away the destination.
scenery.rejection: It is a floor. It is doing floor things.

name: The Arcades
description: A row of shuttered booths along the hall's flank — a market once, by the counters. The shutters are down and the dust is even. Echoes here take a while to come back.

name: The Tea Corner
description: The light from across the hall, arrived at: a small tea stall, swept and warm, kettle on. A robot called the Steeper keeps the ritual — cups set for four, rotated daily, never used. It pours for you before you ask.

name: Engineering Access C
description: A stairwell landing behind a heavy door, all warning stripes and handrails. The air smells of grease and ozone. Machinery thuds somewhere below at the pace of a calm heart.

name: The Pump Gallery
description: A long gallery of pumps moving the ship's water, each one humming a slightly different note. Pipe runs cross overhead in numbered bundles. A pipe near the entrance is labeled TEMPORARY in lettering that has been repainted at least nine times. The maintenance log hangs on a chain, current to this morning.
secret: The maintenance log's entries continue uninterrupted through the emergency hibernation — whoever or whatever keeps the log never slept.

scenery.description: Centuries of entries in dozens of hands, then one hand, very regular, for a long time now. Today's entry: 'Pumps nominal. Visitor.'
scenery.rejection: The log is chained to the rail, and the chain is also in the log.

name: The Reliquary Walk
description: A corridor that lost its argument with a collection. Artifacts shelve every wall floor to ceiling — instruments, garments, tools, toys, urns — each tagged in the Catalog's cramped hand, many tags amended by later, crosser hands. Overhead, suspended exhibits sway very slightly with the ship. You walk single file because history has the rest of the width.

scenery.description: A typical tag: 'Ceremonial spanner, Third Era. — Disputed. — Dispute withdrawn. — Dispute reinstated by new management.'
scenery.rejection: The tags are part of the collection now. There's a tag that says so.

name: The Reliquary Heart
description: The chamber the Walk was protecting: the collection's center, where the oldest things keep each other company. A launch-day helmet rests on a plinth under the only deliberate light. The Catalog's terminal stands at the center aisle, lens raised, already aware of you. Shelves recede into the dark in every direction, full past counting.
secret: The Catalog has an accessions entry for the player: item class 'returning artifact, provenance pending.' It has been holding a shelf space.

scenery.description: The shelves are labeled by era, then by faction, then by a third system that appears to be emotional. The transitions are not marked.
scenery.rejection: The Catalog clears its lens pointedly. Handling is by appointment.
